No requirement is invented; every story traces to an SRS FR. -->

# Product Backlog — RAG Refinement System

**Phase:** 6 — Sprint Planning (GitHub Issues path, Mode A, manifest-only)
**Date:** 2026-06-06
**Gate:** SPRINT READY; 100% FR -> story coverage
**Method:** Epics anchored to HLD components -> INVEST user stories -> WSJF prioritization (PRD Section 11) -> PERT 3-point estimation -> Sprint 1 composition.

---

## 1. Estimation & Prioritization Conventions

### 1.1 PERT 3-point estimate

For every story:

```
Expected effort  E = (O + 4M + P) / 6
Std deviation    SD = (P - O) / 6
Story Points     SP = nearest Fibonacci value to E (1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 13)
```

`O` = optimistic, `M` = most-likely, `P` = pessimistic (all in ideal engineer-days). SP is the Fibonacci rounding of the PERT expected value used for sprint capacity math. A story whose `E` exceeds an 8-day band is split (INVEST "Small").

### 1.2 WSJF

`WSJF = Cost-of-Delay / Job-Size`, where `Cost-of-Delay (CoD) = User-Business Value + Time-Criticality + Risk-Reduction/Opportunity-Enablement` (each 1-10), per PRD Section 11. Story-level WSJF inherits the parent FR's PRD WSJF where the PRD scored that FR; foundation/enabler stories that the PRD did not score individually (schema, ops, security plumbing) are scored here on the same rubric and marked `(planning)`. Higher WSJF = sequence sooner **within** a dependency-feasible set. Dependency order overrides WSJF (PRD Section 11 note).

### 1.3 Definition of Done (global DoD)

Applied to every story unless a story overrides it. Validated by the consensus DoD AHP (see `sprint_verdict.json`):

1. Code merged to the integration branch; lint + type-check + unit tests green in CI (AGREED CONTRACT: devops gate).
2. Unit tests cover the acceptance criterion; P0-risk stories (router, targeted-retrieval filter, citation accuracy) carry deterministic golden fixtures (AGREED CONTRACT: test-management).
3. Acceptance criterion (SRS AC-NNN) demonstrably passes.
4. API-touching stories conform to `openapi.yaml` (do-not-modify); errors are RFC-7807 (SRS-NFR-010).
5. No hardcoded secrets; secrets via 12-factor env (AGREED CONTRACT: devops; SRS-NFR-007).
6. Docstrings present; no inline narration comments (house rule 12).
7. Data-handling stories satisfy DPDP mapping (tenant scoping, x-pii, erasure cascade) where applicable (SRS-NFR-014).
8. Traceability updated: story -> SRS-FR -> AC recorded in `fr_story_traceability.json`.

## 2. Epics (anchored to HLD components)

| Epic ID | Epic | HLD anchor | FRs covered | Kano theme |
|---------|------|------------|-------------|------------|
| **EPIC-STO** | Storage & Data Model (foundation) | HLD §4.1/§4.2/§4.3, ADR-2/ADR-10 | FR-004 (+ schema enabling FR-003/006/024/025) | Must-be |
| **EPIC-OPS** | Observability & Ops (foundation) | HLD §3, §9, NFR-009; AGREED CONTRACT devops | NFR-009 infra enabling all; FR-019/020/022 | Must-be / Performance |
| **EPIC-ING** | Ingestion | HLD §3.1, ADR-4/ADR-6 | FR-001, FR-002, FR-003, FR-017, FR-023 | Must-be |
| **EPIC-RTR** | Router | HLD §3.2, §7.1, ADR-1/ADR-3 | FR-005, FR-009, FR-015 | Performance |
| **EPIC-RAG** | Retrieval & Generation | HLD §4.3, §6, §7.3, ADR-8 | FR-006, FR-007, FR-008, FR-011, FR-012, FR-013, FR-016, FR-018 | Must-be / Delighter |
| **EPIC-API** | Enterprise API | HLD §7.2, ADR-5/ADR-7 | FR-010, FR-014, FR-021, FR-024 | Performance |
| **EPIC-FE** | Frontend (personal tool) | HLD §1.1, ADR-9; screen_inventory.md | FR-007, FR-008, FR-011, FR-012, FR-018, FR-024 (UI) | Delighter |
| **EPIC-SEC** | Security & Compliance (DPDP/CERT-In) | HLD §7.4, §8, NFR-007/008/014/015 | FR-025, FR-026, FR-027, FR-028, FR-029 | Must-be (enterprise) |

8 epics. Storage and Ops are the foundation epics (schema/ops first, per the foundation-first sprint rule).

---

## 3. INVEST User Stories

> Each story: id, epic, title, role/want/so-that, linked SRS-FR, acceptance criterion (SRS AC), WSJF (CoD / Job-Size), PERT (O/M/P -> E -> SP). Global DoD (Section 1.3) applies.

---

### EPIC-STO — Storage & Data Model (foundation)

#### STORY-001 — PostgreSQL structure schema + migrations
- **Epic:** EPIC-STO
- **As a** backend engineer **I want** the `documents` and `sections` tables (L1/L2 hierarchy, tenant_id, page_start/page_end, summary) with versioned migrations **so that** the router and document-management surfaces have an ACID structure store (ADR-10).
- **FR:** enables SRS-FR-003, SRS-FR-024 (schema substrate); **AC:** AC-003 (hierarchy queryable), AC-024 (tenant-scoped).
- **WSJF:** CoD 24 (Value 8 + TC 8 + RR/OE 8) / Size 3 = **8.0** (planning — dependency root)
- **PERT:** O=2 / M=3 / P=6 -> E=3.3 -> **SP 3**
- **DoD+:** migrations are forward + rollback; snake_case tables/columns (house rule 1); tenant_id on every row.

#### STORY-002 — Qdrant collection + payload schema (chunk vectors)
- **Epic:** EPIC-STO
- **As a** data engineer **I want** the Qdrant collection provisioned with payload `{chunk_id, section_id, doc_id, page, tenant_id}` and the section_id/tenant_id payload index **so that** targeted retrieval can filter by section and tenant (ADR-2, AGREED CONTRACT db-engineer).
- **FR:** SRS-FR-004; **AC:** AC-004.
- **WSJF:** CoD 21 (8+7+6) / Size 4 = **5.3** (PRD FR-004)
- **PERT:** O=2 / M=3 / P=5 -> E=3.2 -> **SP 3**
- **DoD+:** payload index on `section_id` and `tenant_id`; dev=ChromaDB / prod=Qdrant parity documented.

#### STORY-003 — Embedding + upsert pipeline (OpenAI te-3-small primary)
- **Epic:** EPIC-STO
- **As a** data engineer **I want** chunk embedding (text-embedding-3-small) and idempotent Qdrant upsert keyed on content hash **so that** re-upload reuses doc_id and chunks are indexed exactly once (ADR-6, AGREED CONTRACT data-engineer).
- **FR:** SRS-FR-004; **AC:** AC-004, AC-001 (dedup path).
- **WSJF:** CoD 21 (8+7+6) / Size 4 = **5.3** (PRD FR-004)
- **PERT:** O=2 / M=4 / P=7 -> E=4.2 -> **SP 5**
- **DoD+:** idempotent on content hash; embedding provider key via env only.

---

### EPIC-OPS — Observability & Ops (foundation)

#### STORY-004 — Service skeleton: Dockerfile + /health + /ready
- **Epic:** EPIC-OPS
- **As a** devops engineer **I want** each service to ship a Dockerfile, a liveness `/health`, and a dependency-reachability `/ready` **so that** the platform is deployable and degradation is observable (AGREED CONTRACT devops; SRS-NFR-009).
- **FR:** SRS-NFR-009 (infra enabling all FRs); **AC:** AC for NFR-009 (HLD §9 `GET /health`, `GET /ready`).
- **WSJF:** CoD 22 (8+8+6) / Size 3 = **7.3** (planning — enables CI for every story)
- **PERT:** O=1 / M=2 / P=4 -> E=2.2 -> **SP 2**
- **DoD+:** `/ready` returns 503 when a dependency is down (feeds NFR-013 degradation UI).

#### STORY-005 — CI pipeline: lint + type + unit + SAST + secrets scan
- **Epic:** EPIC-OPS
- **As a** devops engineer **I want** a CI workflow running lint, type-check, unit tests, SAST, and a secrets scan on every push **so that** the AGREED CONTRACT quality + security gates block bad merges (AGREED CONTRACT devops + sast/secrets).
- **FR:** SRS-NFR-007 (secrets gate), SRS-NFR-010 (contract test hook); **AC:** CI blocks on hardcoded key.
- **WSJF:** CoD 23 (8+9+6) / Size 3 = **7.7** (planning — gate for all stories)
- **PERT:** O=1 / M=2 / P=5 -> E=2.3 -> **SP 2**
- **DoD+:** SAST + secrets scan block on finding; Python + TS stacks configured.

#### STORY-006 — LangSmith tracing wiring
- **Epic:** EPIC-OPS
- **As an** AI engineer **I want** routing and generation runs traced in LangSmith **so that** accuracy/drift/usage is observable per AGREED CONTRACT (ai-model-testing) and feeds the analytics dashboard.
- **FR:** SRS-NFR-009; enables SRS-FR-020; **AC:** AC-020 (metrics sourced from LangSmith).
- **WSJF:** CoD 16 (6+4+6) / Size 3 = **5.3** (planning)
- **PERT:** O=1 / M=2 / P=4 -> E=2.2 -> **SP 2**

#### STORY-007 — Token-savings / accuracy / usage analytics dashboard
- **Epic:** EPIC-OPS
- **As a** product owner **I want** a dashboard of token savings, accuracy, and usage **so that** the core token-reduction thesis is measurable (PRD §9.3).
- **FR:** SRS-FR-020; **AC:** AC-020.
- **WSJF:** CoD 12 (5+3+4) / Size 5 = **2.4** (planning — P2)
- **PERT:** O=3 / M=5 / P=9 -> E=5.3 -> **SP 5**

### EPIC-ING — Ingestion

#### STORY-008 — PDF upload + text/structure extraction (POST /v1/documents)
- **Epic:** EPIC-ING
- **As a** user **I want** to upload a PDF and have its text + structure extracted **so that** I can ask questions over it; identical re-upload returns the same doc_id (deduplicated).
- **FR:** SRS-FR-001; **AC:** AC-001.
- **WSJF:** CoD 21 (8+8+5) / Size 3 = **7.0** (PRD FR-001)
- **PERT:** O=2 / M=3 / P=5 -> E=3.2 -> **SP 3**
- **DoD+:** `IngestResponse{doc_id, toc, ingest_status, deduplicated}`; idempotent on content hash.

#### STORY-009 — Native TOC extraction (Scenario A, get_toc)
- **Epic:** EPIC-ING
- **As a** user **I want** the native bookmark TOC extracted with level/title/page ranges **so that** the router has the document structure to route over (ADR-4, Scenario A).
- **FR:** SRS-FR-002; **AC:** AC-002 (bookmark path).
- **WSJF:** CoD 27 (9+9+9) / Size 5 = **5.4** (PRD FR-002)
- **PERT:** O=2 / M=3 / P=6 -> E=3.3 -> **SP 3**

#### STORY-010 — Pseudo-TOC LLM fallback (Scenarios B/C)
- **Epic:** EPIC-ING
- **As a** user **I want** an LLM-generated pseudo-TOC when no bookmarks exist **so that** structureless PDFs are still routable (ADR-4, Scenarios B/C).
- **FR:** SRS-FR-002; **AC:** AC-002 (pseudo-TOC path), AC-017 boundary (`fallback_only`).
- **WSJF:** CoD 27 (9+9+9) / Size 5 = **5.4** (PRD FR-002)
- **PERT:** O=3 / M=5 / P=8 -> E=5.2 -> **SP 5**

#### STORY-011 — Section-aware 3-level chunking (no cross-section chunks)
- **Epic:** EPIC-ING
- **As a** data engineer **I want** Document->Section->Chunk chunking where no chunk crosses a section boundary **so that** every chunk carries exactly one section_id for clean targeted retrieval (AGREED CONTRACT db-engineer).
- **FR:** SRS-FR-003; **AC:** AC-003.
- **WSJF:** CoD 22 (8+7+7) / Size 4 = **5.5** (PRD FR-003)
- **PERT:** O=2 / M=4 / P=7 -> E=4.2 -> **SP 5**

#### STORY-012 — OCR fallback for scanned PDFs (Azure Document Intelligence)
- **Epic:** EPIC-ING
- **As a** user **I want** scanned/image PDFs processed via OCR (or `ocr=true`) **so that** non-digital documents are ingestible (ADR-4 P3).
- **FR:** SRS-FR-017; **AC:** AC-017.
- **WSJF:** CoD 14 (6+4+4) / Size 4 = **3.5** (planning — P1, tiered fallback)
- **PERT:** O=3 / M=5 / P=9 -> E=5.3 -> **SP 5**

#### STORY-013 — Multilingual embedding fallback (BGE-M3)
- **Epic:** EPIC-ING
- **As a** user **I want** non-English documents embedded via BGE-M3 **so that** multilingual docs are routable/retrievable in their source language (ADR-6).
- **FR:** SRS-FR-023; **AC:** AC-023.
- **WSJF:** CoD 12 (5+3+4) / Size 4 = **3.0** (planning — P2)
- **PERT:** O=2 / M=4 / P=7 -> E=4.2 -> **SP 5**

### EPIC-RTR — Router

#### STORY-014 — LangGraph router node (Claude 3 Haiku, RouterOutput contract)
- **Epic:** EPIC-RTR
- **As an** AI engineer **I want** a LangGraph router emitting `{relevant_sections[], page_ranges[], confidence[], fallback, routing_time_ms}` validated against the RouterOutput contract **so that** queries are scoped to sections before search (ADR-1/ADR-3, HLD §7.1).
- **FR:** SRS-FR-005; **AC:** AC-005.
- **WSJF:** CoD 29 (10+9+10) / Size 5 = **5.8** (PRD FR-005)
- **PERT:** O=3 / M=5 / P=9 -> E=5.3 -> **SP 5**
- **DoD+:** P0-risk golden TOC fixtures; LLM response rejected if non-conforming -> deterministic fallback (ADV-004); routing_time_ms in [100,300] (SRS-NFR-001).

#### STORY-015 — Confidence-gated fallback to full-document RAG
- **Epic:** EPIC-RTR
- **As a** user **I want** the system to fall back to full-document retrieval when all section confidences are below threshold **so that** I still get an answer with `fallback=true` rather than a wrong scope (HLD §3.2, OAQ-6).
- **FR:** SRS-FR-009; **AC:** AC-009.
- **WSJF:** CoD 23 (7+7+9) / Size 3 = **7.7** (PRD FR-009)
- **PERT:** O=2 / M=3 / P=5 -> E=3.2 -> **SP 3**
- **DoD+:** P0-risk; fallback rate measured < 20% on the golden eval set (SRS-NFR-005).

#### STORY-016 — Query rewriting (pre-router clarification)
- **Epic:** EPIC-RTR
- **As a** user **I want** vague queries rewritten before routing **so that** ambiguous questions route to the right sections (HLD §3.2 pre-router step).
- **FR:** SRS-FR-015; **AC:** AC-015.
- **WSJF:** CoD 13 (5+4+4) / Size 3 = **4.3** (planning — P1)
- **PERT:** O=2 / M=3 / P=6 -> E=3.3 -> **SP 3**

---

### EPIC-RAG — Retrieval & Generation

#### STORY-017 — Targeted retrieval filtered to router-selected sections
- **Epic:** EPIC-RAG
- **As a** backend engineer **I want** Qdrant search filtered to `section_id IN (router-selected) AND tenant_id = caller` **so that** retrieval is scoped (the core "scope before search" mechanism) and no out-of-section chunk leaks (HLD §4.3, §7.5).
- **FR:** SRS-FR-006; **AC:** AC-006.
- **WSJF:** CoD 26 (10+8+8) / Size 5 = **5.2** (PRD FR-006)
- **PERT:** O=2 / M=3 / P=6 -> E=3.3 -> **SP 3**
- **DoD+:** P0-risk; deterministic golden section-set fixtures; tenant filter mandatory (IDOR guard).

#### STORY-018 — Answer generation + SSE streaming (POST /v1/answer)
- **Epic:** EPIC-RAG
- **As a** user **I want** answers streamed token-by-token via SSE ending with a final event `{answer, citations[], routing{}}` **so that** I see responses as they generate (HLD §7.3).
- **FR:** SRS-FR-007, SRS-FR-018; **AC:** AC-007, AC-018.
- **WSJF:** CoD 18 (7+7+4) / Size 4 = **4.5** (PRD FR-007) [FR-018 5.0]
- **PERT:** O=3 / M=5 / P=8 -> E=5.2 -> **SP 5**
- **DoD+:** mid-stream error -> `event: error` -> Problem (AC-ADV-002); SSE contract tested.

#### STORY-019 — Source citation validation (interval-tree, section+page)
- **Epic:** EPIC-RAG
- **As a** user **I want** every answer to cite section title + page range, each cited page within a router-selected section **so that** I can trust and verify the answer (HLD §6 interval tree).
- **FR:** SRS-FR-008; **AC:** AC-008.
- **WSJF:** CoD 22 (8+7+7) / Size 3 = **7.3** (PRD FR-008)
- **PERT:** O=2 / M=3 / P=6 -> E=3.3 -> **SP 3**
- **DoD+:** P0-risk; citation-accuracy fixtures; interval-tree validates page in selected section.

#### STORY-020 — Routing confidence display data (RoutingSummary.confidence reduction)
- **Epic:** EPIC-RAG
- **As a** user **I want** a single routing-confidence value derived as `max(routing.confidence[])` **so that** I can gauge how sure the router was (GRC-002 reduction rule).
- **FR:** SRS-FR-011; **AC:** AC-011.
- **WSJF:** CoD 16 (6+5+5) / Size 2 = **8.0** (PRD FR-011)
- **PERT:** O=1 / M=2 / P=3 -> E=2.0 -> **SP 2**

#### STORY-021 — "Why did you look here?" explainability data path
- **Epic:** EPIC-RAG
- **As a** user **I want** the rationale plus a considered-sections list (with rejected sections, scores, page ranges) **so that** I understand why those sections were searched (GRC-001 client-side TOC join, HLD §7.1).
- **FR:** SRS-FR-012; **AC:** AC-012.
- **WSJF:** CoD 20 (8+6+6) / Size 3 = **6.7** (PRD FR-012)
- **PERT:** O=2 / M=3 / P=5 -> E=3.2 -> **SP 3**

#### STORY-022 — Hybrid search (TOC routing + BM25 + vector)
- **Epic:** EPIC-RAG
- **As a** user **I want** hybrid search fusing TOC-scope with BM25 and vector results **so that** keyword-exact and semantic matches both surface within routed sections (HLD §3.2).
- **FR:** SRS-FR-013; **AC:** AC-013.
- **WSJF:** CoD 14 (6+4+4) / Size 5 = **2.8** (planning — P1)
- **PERT:** O=3 / M=5 / P=9 -> E=5.3 -> **SP 5**

#### STORY-023 — Optional Cohere Rerank-3 (per-request toggle)
- **Epic:** EPIC-RAG
- **As an** integrator **I want** Cohere Rerank-3 applied post-route when `rerank=true` (default false) **so that** I can trade cost for a precision lift (ADR-8).
- **FR:** SRS-FR-016; **AC:** AC-016.
- **WSJF:** CoD 16 (6+4+6) / Size 3 = **5.3** (PRD FR-016)
- **PERT:** O=2 / M=3 / P=5 -> E=3.2 -> **SP 3**

---

### EPIC-API — Enterprise API

#### STORY-024 — Routing-only endpoint POST /v1/route (no generation)
- **Epic:** EPIC-API
- **As an** enterprise integrator **I want** `POST /v1/route` returning `RouteResponse` (routing JSON only, no generation) **so that** my existing RAG narrows its retrieval scope before vector search (HLD §7.2 invariant, ADR-5/ADR-7).
- **FR:** SRS-FR-010; **AC:** AC-010.
- **WSJF:** CoD 21 (9+6+6) / Size 5 = **4.2** (PRD FR-010)
- **PERT:** O=2 / M=3 / P=6 -> E=3.3 -> **SP 3**
- **DoD+:** graph terminates after confidence gate (no generation LLM call); API-key auth + rate-limit; oneOf document_id|document_ids contract tests (AC-ADV-001).

#### STORY-025 — Multi-document routing (document_ids[] fan-out / map-reduce)
- **Epic:** EPIC-API
- **As an** enterprise integrator **I want** to route across multiple related documents (<=25) with a global confidence sort **so that** cross-document questions scope correctly (HLD OAQ-4). Routing-only for MVP (ADV-006).
- **FR:** SRS-FR-014; **AC:** AC-014.
- **WSJF:** CoD 15 (7+4+4) / Size 5 = **3.0** (planning — P1)
- **PERT:** O=3 / M=5 / P=9 -> E=5.3 -> **SP 5**

#### STORY-026 — Document library + management surface (list/get, IDOR-guarded)
- **Epic:** EPIC-API
- **As a** user **I want** `GET /v1/documents` (paginated, tenant-scoped) and `GET /v1/documents/{id}` (IDOR-guarded) **so that** I can browse and inspect my documents (HLD §4).
- **FR:** SRS-FR-024; **AC:** AC-024.
- **WSJF:** CoD 17 (7+5+5) / Size 3 = **5.7** (planning — P2 surface)
- **PERT:** O=2 / M=3 / P=5 -> E=3.2 -> **SP 3**
- **DoD+:** 403 on cross-tenant access; pagination per house rule 6.

#### STORY-027 — Python + JavaScript SDKs + published API docs
- **Epic:** EPIC-API
- **As an** enterprise integrator **I want** Python/JS SDKs and API docs generated from `openapi.yaml` **so that** I can integrate without hand-writing HTTP calls (PRD §9.3).
- **FR:** SRS-FR-021; **AC:** AC-021.
- **WSJF:** CoD 11 (5+3+3) / Size 5 = **2.2** (planning — P2)
- **PERT:** O=3 / M=5 / P=9 -> E=5.3 -> **SP 5**

### EPIC-FE — Frontend (personal tool)

#### STORY-028 — Chat + SSE stream UI (ChatStream, MessageBubble, composer)
- **Epic:** EPIC-FE
- **As a** user **I want** a chat interface that streams the answer token-by-token **so that** I can ask questions and watch the response build (screen_inventory Chat; ADR-9).
- **FR:** SRS-FR-007, SRS-FR-018 (UI); **AC:** AC-007, AC-018.
- **WSJF:** CoD 18 (7+7+4) / Size 4 = **4.5** (PRD FR-007)
- **PERT:** O=3 / M=5 / P=8 -> E=5.2 -> **SP 5**
- **DoD+:** mid-stream `event: error` surfaced role=alert (AC-ADV-002).

#### STORY-029 — CitationCard + ConfidenceMeter + ExplainabilityPanel
- **Epic:** EPIC-FE
- **As a** user **I want** citations, a confidence meter, and a "why here?" panel rendered on each answer **so that** every answer is interpretable (the 3 core differentiators; reconciliation_matrix rows 1-3).
- **FR:** SRS-FR-008, SRS-FR-011, SRS-FR-012 (UI); **AC:** AC-008, AC-011, AC-012; SRS-NFR-011.
- **WSJF:** CoD 22 (8+7+7) / Size 5 = **4.4** (cluster avg of FR-008/011/012)
- **PERT:** O=3 / M=5 / P=8 -> E=5.2 -> **SP 5**
- **DoD+:** APCA Lc>=60 body; state by label+icon+bar (not color alone) per SRS-NFR-011; ConfidenceMeter `aria-valuenow=max(confidence[])` (GRC-002); panel via client-side TOC join (GRC-001).

#### STORY-030 — Upload + Document Library UI (dropzone, cards, badges)
- **Epic:** EPIC-FE
- **As a** user **I want** an upload dropzone and a document library with status badges **so that** I can ingest and manage my documents (screen_inventory UploadLibrary).
- **FR:** SRS-FR-001, SRS-FR-024 (UI); **AC:** AC-001, AC-024.
- **WSJF:** CoD 17 (7+5+5) / Size 4 = **4.3** (planning)
- **PERT:** O=2 / M=4 / P=7 -> E=4.2 -> **SP 5**
- **DoD+:** badge map `indexed->Indexed`, `fallback_only->Fallback-only`, `ephemeral->Ephemeral` (GRC-003).

#### STORY-031 — TOC sidebar + DocumentView (tree navigation, fallback banner)
- **Epic:** EPIC-FE
- **As a** user **I want** a TOC sidebar showing the section hierarchy and a fallback banner **so that** I can navigate structure and see when the system degraded (screen_inventory DocumentView/FallbackIndicator).
- **FR:** SRS-FR-002, SRS-FR-003, SRS-FR-009 (UI); **AC:** AC-002, AC-003, AC-009; SRS-NFR-013.
- **WSJF:** CoD 16 (6+5+5) / Size 4 = **4.0** (planning)
- **PERT:** O=2 / M=4 / P=7 -> E=4.2 -> **SP 5**
- **DoD+:** TocSidebar collapses to drawer < 768px; FallbackBanner reads routing.fallback / fallback_only / 503.

#### STORY-032 — Feedback capture (like/dislike on answers)
- **Epic:** EPIC-FE
- **As a** user **I want** to like/dislike an answer **so that** my feedback is persisted against the query_id to improve routing (PRD §9.3 Phase 4 roadmap).
- **FR:** SRS-FR-019; **AC:** AC-019.
- **WSJF:** CoD 10 (4+3+3) / Size 3 = **3.3** (planning — P2)
- **PERT:** O=1 / M=2 / P=4 -> E=2.2 -> **SP 2**

### EPIC-SEC — Security & Compliance (DPDP / CERT-In)

#### STORY-033 — DPDP erasure: DELETE /v1/documents/{id} (cascade + 503)
- **Epic:** EPIC-SEC
- **As a** data subject **I want** `DELETE /v1/documents/{id}` to tombstone the document and cascade-erase its sections + chunk vectors **so that** my right to erasure (DPDP §8) is honored; a subsequent GET returns 404 (HLD §7.4, OAQ-2).
- **FR:** SRS-FR-025; **AC:** AC-025.
- **WSJF:** CoD 23 (7+7+9) / Size 3 = **7.7** (PRD FR-025)
- **PERT:** O=2 / M=3 / P=6 -> E=3.3 -> **SP 3**
- **DoD+:** erasure cascade idempotent; add 503 + Retry-After (ADV-002); outbox sweep idempotent on retry.

#### STORY-034 — DPDP access: GET /v1/documents/{id}/data + x-pii inventory
- **Epic:** EPIC-SEC
- **As a** data subject **I want** `GET /v1/documents/{id}/data` to return the personal data held plus an `x-pii` field inventory **so that** my right to access (DPDP §8) and PII transparency (DPDP §4) are honored (HLD §7.4).
- **FR:** SRS-FR-026, SRS-FR-029; **AC:** AC-026, AC-029.
- **WSJF:** CoD 18 (7+5+6) / Size 3 = **6.0** (planning — DPDP enabler)
- **PERT:** O=2 / M=3 / P=5 -> E=3.2 -> **SP 3**
- **DoD+:** `DataAccessExport.pii_fields[]` enumerates x-pii fields; x-pii annotated in schema consumption.

#### STORY-035 — No-retention (ephemeral) mode purge after answer
- **Epic:** EPIC-SEC
- **As a** privacy-sensitive user **I want** `no_retention=true` to purge raw upload, chunks, and sections after the answer **so that** no document data persists (DPDP data-minimization, HLD §7.4, OAQ-1).
- **FR:** SRS-FR-027; **AC:** AC-027.
- **WSJF:** CoD 22 (7+6+9) / Size 4 = **5.5** (PRD FR-027)
- **PERT:** O=2 / M=4 / P=7 -> E=4.2 -> **SP 5**
- **DoD+:** post-answer purge verified absent in Qdrant AND PostgreSQL; reconciles with idempotent re-upload (OAQ-1).

#### STORY-036 — India data-residency configuration
- **Epic:** EPIC-SEC
- **As an** India tenant **I want** `residency_region=IN` to store my document data on India-region stores **so that** DPDP data-residency is satisfied (HLD §4.2, OAQ-5).
- **FR:** SRS-FR-028; **AC:** AC-028.
- **WSJF:** CoD 16 (6+5+5) / Size 4 = **4.0** (planning — P1)
- **PERT:** O=2 / M=4 / P=7 -> E=4.2 -> **SP 5**

#### STORY-037 — CERT-In 6-hour incident-reporting hook + audit logging
- **Epic:** EPIC-SEC
- **As a** security/compliance owner **I want** a CERT-In-format 6-hour incident-reporting hook and tamper-evident audit logging on security events **so that** IT Act "reasonable security" + CERT-In obligations are met (SRS-NFR-007; AGREED CONTRACT security-compliance-mapper).
- **FR:** SRS-NFR-007 (CERT-In 6h, audit); enables SRS-FR-025/026/027 audit trail; **AC:** NFR-007 6h reporting hook fires on security events.
- **WSJF:** CoD 23 (8+9+6) / Size 4 = **5.8** (planning — regulatory, auto-created Sprint 1)
- **PERT:** O=2 / M=4 / P=7 -> E=4.2 -> **SP 5**
- **DoD+:** CERT-In incident format; audit log immutable/append-only; no PII in logs (house rule 3).

#### STORY-038 — Auth: API keys (enterprise, rotation) + OAuth2/JWT (personal)
- **Epic:** EPIC-SEC
- **As a** platform owner **I want** API-key auth with rotation for the enterprise API and OAuth2/JWT bearer for the personal tool **so that** both surfaces are authenticated (ADR-7, SRS-NFR-015).
- **FR:** SRS-NFR-015 (enabling every authenticated FR); **AC:** AC-024 IDOR guard depends on this.
- **WSJF:** CoD 24 (8+8+8) / Size 4 = **6.0** (planning — auth substrate)
- **PERT:** O=2 / M=4 / P=7 -> E=4.2 -> **SP 5**
- **DoD+:** API-key rotation; JWT validation; rate-limit on authenticated ops (ADV-003); session-fixation review hook.

## 4. Backlog Summary

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Epics | 8 |
| Stories | 38 |
| Total SP (PERT-rounded) | 147 |
| FR coverage | 29 / 29 SRS-FR (100%) + SRS-NFR-007/009/010/011/013/015 carried as enabler stories |
| P0 foundation stories | STORY-001..005, 008, 009, 011, 014, 015, 017, 019 |
| Auto-created compliance (Sprint 1) | STORY-033 (DPDP erasure), STORY-034 (DPDP access + x-pii), STORY-037 (CERT-In) |

WSJF guides sequence within dependency-feasible sets; the P0 foundation chain (STO + OPS + core ING/RTR/RAG) lands first regardless of raw WSJF (PRD Section 11 note). See `sprint_1_backlog.md` for the Sprint-1 selection and `fr_story_traceability.json` for full FR -> story coverage.
